CGAL::Eigen_solver_traits.name: CGAL::Eigen_solver_traits
CGAL::Eigen_solver_traits.brief: The class Eigen_solver_traits provides an interface to the sparse solvers of Eigen.
CGAL::Eigen_solver_traits.detailed: Eigen version 3.1 (or later) must be available on the system. Is model of SparseLinearAlgebraWithFactorTraits_d NormalEquationSparseLinearAlgebraTraits_d EigenSolverT A sparse solver of Eigen. The default solver is the iterative bi-conjugate gradient stabilized solver Eigen::BiCGSTAB for double . CGAL::Eigen_sparse_matrix <T> CGAL::Eigen_sparse_symmetric_matrix <T> CGAL::Eigen_vector <T> https://eigen.tuxfamily.org/index.php?title=Main_Page Instantiation Example The instantiation of this class assumes an Eigen sparse solver is provided. Here are few examples: typedef CGAL::Eigen_sparse_matrix<double>::EigenType EigenMatrix; //iterative general solver typedef CGAL::Eigen_solver_traits< Eigen::BiCGSTAB<EigenMatrix> > Iterative_general_solver; //iterative symmetric solver typedef CGAL::Eigen_solver_traits< Eigen::ConjugateGradient<EigenMatrix> > Iterative_symmetric_solver; //direct symmetric solver typedef CGAL::Eigen_solver_traits< Eigen::SimplicialCholesky<EigenMatrix> > Direct_symmetric_solver;

members.name: Matrix
members.brief: If T is Eigen::ConjugateGradient<M> or Eigen::SimplicialCholesky<M> , Matrix is CGAL::Eigen_sparse_symmetric_matrix <T> , and CGAL::Eigen_sparse_matrix <T> otherwise.

members.name: linear_solver
members.brief: Solve the sparse linear system $ A \times X = B $ .
members.detailed: Return true on success. The solution is then $ (1/D) \times X $ . A.row_dimension() == B.dimension(). A.column_dimension() == X.dimension().

members.name: factor
members.brief: Factorize the sparse matrix $ A $ .
members.detailed: This factorization is used in linear_solver() to solve the system for different right-hand side vectors. See linear_solver() for the description of $ D $ . true if the factorization is successful and false otherwise.

members.name: linear_solver
members.brief: Solve the sparse linear system $ A \times X = B$ , with $ A $ being the matrix provided in factor() .
members.detailed: true if the solver is successful and false otherwise.

members.name: normal_equation_factor
members.brief: Factorize the sparse matrix $ A^t \times A$ , where $ A^t $ is the transpose matrix of $ A $ .
members.detailed: This factorization is used in normal_equation_solver() to solve the system for different right-hand side vectors. true if the factorization is successful and false otherwise.

members.name: normal_equation_solver
members.brief: Solve the sparse linear system $ A^t \times A \times X = A^t \times B $ , with $ A $ being the matrix provided in # normal_equation_factor() , and $ A^t $ its transpose matrix.
members.detailed: true if the solver is successful and false otherwise.

members.name: normal_equation_solver
members.brief: Equivalent to a call to normal_equation_factor(A) followed by a call to normal_equation_solver(B, X) .

members.name: set_coef
members.brief: Write access to a matrix coefficient: a_ij <- val.
members.detailed: Users can optimize calls to this function by setting 'new_coef' to true if the coefficient does not already exist in the matrix. For symmetric matrices, Eigen_sparse_matrix only stores the lower triangle and set_coef() does nothing if (i, j) belongs to the upper triangle. 0 <= i < row_dimension() . 0 <= j < column_dimension() .
members.argsstring: (std::size_t i_, std::size_t j_, T val, bool new_coef=false)

members.name: add_coef
members.brief: Write access to a matrix coefficient: a_ij <- a_ij + val.
members.detailed: For symmetric matrices, Eigen_sparse_matrix only stores the lower triangle add_coef() does nothing if (i, j) belongs to the upper triangle. 0 <= i < row_dimension() . 0 <= j < column_dimension() .

members.name: get_coef
members.brief: Read access to a matrix coefficient.
members.detailed: Complexity: $O(log(n))$ if the matrix is already built. $O(n)$ if the matrix is not built. n being the number of entries in the matrix. 0 <= i < row_dimension() . 0 <= j < column_dimension() .

CGAL::Mixed_integer_program_traits.name: CGAL::Mixed_integer_program_traits
CGAL::Mixed_integer_program_traits.brief: The class CGAL::Mixed_integer_program_traits provides an interface for formulating and solving (constrained or unconstrained) mixed integer programs.
CGAL::Mixed_integer_program_traits.detailed: It can also be used for general linear programs. The solve() function is virtual and thus this class cannot be instantiated directly. Client code should use the inherited classes, i.e., CGAL::GLPK_mixed_integer_program_traits or CGAL::SCIP_mixed_integer_program_traits . Alternatively, use CGAL::Mixed_integer_program_traits as a base to derive a new model (using e.g., CBC , Gurobi for better performance). Is model of MixedIntegerProgramTraits

CGAL::OSQP_quadratic_program_traits.name: CGAL::OSQP_quadratic_program_traits
CGAL::OSQP_quadratic_program_traits.detailed: This class provides an interface for formulating and solving constrained or unconstrained quadratic programs using the OSQP library, which must be available on the system. FT number type that FieldNumberType The FT type is provided for convenience. Internally, this FT type is converted to OSQPFloat type that can be set either to float or double . By default, the double type is used. After the optimization is complete, the OSQPFloat type is converted back to FT . See more about OSQPFloat here . Is model of QuadraticProgramTraits

LinearProgramTraits.name: LinearProgramTraits
LinearProgramTraits.brief: A concept that describes the set of methods used to define and solve a linear programming ( lp ) problem of the general form:
LinearProgramTraits.detailed: \begin{eqnarray*}& \mbox{minimize} & \mathbf{q}^{T}\mathbf{x} + r \\ & \mbox{subject to} & \mathbf{l} \leq A\mathbf{x} \leq \mathbf{u} \end{eqnarray*} in $ n $ real variables $ \mathbf{x} = (x_0, \ldots, x_{n-1}) $ and $ m $ constraints. Here, $ \mathbf{q} $ is an $ n $ -dimensional vector (the linear objective function), $ r $ is a constant, $ A $ is an $ m\times n$ matrix (the constraint matrix), $ \mathbf{l} $ is an $ m $ -dimensional vector of lower constraint bounds, where $ l_i \in \mathbb{R} \cup \{-\infty\} $ for all $ i $ , $ \mathbf{u} $ is an $ m $ -dimensional vector of upper constraint bounds, where $ u_i \in \mathbb{R} \cup \{+\infty\} $ for all $ i $ .

members.name: resize
members.brief: Allocates memory for n variables and m constraints in lp .

members.name: set_q
members.brief: Sets the entry qi of lp to value .

members.name: set_r
members.brief: Sets the entry r of lp to value .

members.name: set_A
members.brief: Sets the entry Aij in the row i and column j of the constraint matrix A of lp to value .
members.argsstring: (const std::size_t i, const std::size_t j, const FT value)

members.name: set_l
members.brief: Sets the entry li of lp to value .

members.name: set_u
members.brief: Sets the entry ui of lp to value .

members.name: solve
members.brief: solves the linear program.
members.detailed: Number of values in solution equals to the number n of values in the vector x . OutIterator a model of OutputIterator that accepts values of type FieldNumberType solution an output iterator with the solution a status of the computation success == true

MixedIntegerProgramTraits.name: MixedIntegerProgramTraits
MixedIntegerProgramTraits.brief: Concept describing the set of requirements for (constrained or unconstrained) Mixed Integer Programming (MIP) problems. A model of this concept stores the integer variables, linear objective, and linear constraints (if any) and provides a method to solve the problem.

members.name: MixedIntegerProgramTraits
members.brief: Default constructor.

members.name: create_variable
members.brief: Creates a single variable, adds it to the solver, and returns its pointer.
members.detailed: Memory is managed by the solver and will be automatically released.
members.argsstring: (Variable_type type, FT lb, FT ub, const std::string &name)

members.name: create_variables
members.brief: Creates a set of variables, adds them to the solver, and returns their pointers.
members.detailed: (1) Variables will be given default names, e.g., x0, x1...; (2) Memory is managed by the solver and will be automatically released when the solver is destroyed.

members.name: create_constraint
members.brief: Creates a single linear constraint, adds it to the solver, and returns the pointer.
members.detailed: Memory is managed by the solver and will be automatically released when the solver is destroyed.

members.name: create_constraints
members.brief: Creates a set of linear constraints, adds them to the solver, and returns their pointers.
members.detailed: (1) Constraints will be given default names, e.g., c0, c1... (2) Memory is managed by the solver and will be automatically released when the solver is destroyed.

members.name: create_objective
members.brief: Creates the objective function and returns the pointer.
members.detailed: Memory is managed by the solver and will be automatically released when the solver is destroyed.

members.detailed: Values greater than this value are considered as infinity.

NormalEquationSparseLinearAlgebraTraits_.name: NormalEquationSparseLinearAlgebraTraits_d
NormalEquationSparseLinearAlgebraTraits_.brief: Concept describing the set of requirements for solving the normal equation $ A^t A X = A^t B $ , $ A $ being a matrix, $ At $ its transpose matrix, $ B $ and $ X $ being two vectors.

members.name: Matrix
members.brief: Matrix type model of SparseLinearAlgebraTraits_d::Matrix .

members.name: Vector
members.brief: Vector type model of SparseLinearAlgebraTraits_d::Vector .

members.name: NT
members.brief: Number type.

members.name: NormalEquationSparseLinearAlgebraTraits_d
members.brief: Default constructor.

members.name: normal_equation_factor
members.brief: Factorize the sparse matrix At * A .
members.detailed: This factorization is used in normal_equation_solver() to solve the system for different right-hand side vectors. true if the factorization is successful and false otherwise.

members.name: normal_equation_solver
members.brief: Solve the sparse linear system At * A * X = At * B , with A being the matrix provided in # normal_equation_factor() , and At its transpose matrix.
members.detailed: true if the solver is successful and false otherwise.

members.name: normal_equation_solver
members.brief: Equivalent to a call to normal_equation_factor(A) followed by a call to normal_equation_solver(B,X) .

QuadraticProgramTraits.name: QuadraticProgramTraits
QuadraticProgramTraits.brief: A concept that describes the set of methods used to define and solve a quadratic programming ( qp ) problem of the general form:
QuadraticProgramTraits.detailed: \begin{eqnarray*}& \mbox{minimize} & \frac{1}{2}\mathbf{x}^{T}P\mathbf{x} + \mathbf{q}^{T}\mathbf{x} + r \\ & \mbox{subject to} & \mathbf{l} \leq A\mathbf{x} \leq \mathbf{u} \end{eqnarray*} in $ n $ real variables $ \mathbf{x} = (x_0, \ldots, x_{n-1}) $ and $ m $ constraints. Here, $ P $ is a symmetric positive-semidefinite $ n \times n$ matrix (the quadratic objective function), $ \mathbf{q} $ is an $ n $ -dimensional vector (the linear objective function), $ r $ is a constant, $ A $ is an $ m\times n$ matrix (the constraint matrix), $ \mathbf{l} $ is an $ m $ -dimensional vector of lower constraint bounds, where $ l_i \in \mathbb{R} \cup \{-\infty\} $ for all $ i $ , $ \mathbf{u} $ is an $ m $ -dimensional vector of upper constraint bounds, where $ u_i \in \mathbb{R} \cup \{+\infty\} $ for all $ i $ . Has models CGAL::OSQP_quadratic_program_traits <T>

members.name: resize
members.brief: Allocates memory for n variables and m constraints in qp .

members.name: set_P
members.brief: Sets the entries Pij and Pji of qp to value .
members.argsstring: (const std::size_t i, const std::size_t j, const FT value)

members.name: set_q
members.brief: Sets the entry qi of qp to value .

members.name: set_r
members.brief: Sets the entry r of qp to value .

members.name: set_A
members.brief: Sets the entry Aij in the row i and column j of the constraint matrix A of qp to value .
members.argsstring: (const std::size_t i, const std::size_t j, const FT value)

members.name: set_l
members.brief: Sets the entry li of qp to value .

members.name: set_u
members.brief: Sets the entry ui of qp to value .

members.name: solve
members.brief: solves the quadratic program.
members.detailed: Number of values in solution equals to the number n of values in the vector x . OutIterator a model of OutputIterator that accepts values of type FieldNumberType solution an output iterator with the solution a status of the computation success == true

SparseLinearAlgebraWithFactorTraits_d.name: SparseLinearAlgebraWithFactorTraits_d
SparseLinearAlgebraWithFactorTraits_d.brief: Concept describing the set of requirements for a direct sparse linear system solver with factorization. A model of this concept stores the left-hand matrix (denoted $ A $ ) and provides an additional factorization method to solve the system for different right-hand vectors.

members.name: SparseLinearAlgebraWithFactorTraits_d
members.brief: Default constructor.

members.name: factor
members.brief: Factorize the sparse matrix A .
members.detailed: This factorization is used in SparseLinearAlgebraWithFactorTraits_d::linear_solver() to solve the system for different right-hand side vectors. See SparseLinearAlgebraTraits_d::linear_solver() for the description of D . true if the factorization is successful and false otherwise.

members.name: linear_solver
members.brief: Solve the sparse linear system $ A \times X = B$ , with $ A $ being the matrix provided in SparseLinearAlgebraWithFactorTraits_d::factor() .
members.detailed: true if the solver is successful and false otherwise.

members.name: linear_solver
members.brief: Solve the sparse linear system $ A \times X = B$ , with $ A $ being the matrix provided in SparseLinearAlgebraWithFactorTraits_d::factor() .
members.detailed: true if the solver is successful and false otherwise.

SvdTraits.name: SvdTraits
SvdTraits.brief: The concept SvdTraits describes the linear algebra types and algorithms needed to solve in the least square sense a linear system with a singular value decomposition.

members.name: FT
members.brief: The scalar type.

members.name: Vector
members.brief: The vector type, model of the concept SvdTraits::Vector .

members.name: Matrix
members.brief: The matrix type, model of the concept SvdTraits::Matrix .

members.name: solve
members.brief: Solves the system $ MX=B$ (in the least square sense if $ M$ is not square) using a singular value decomposition and returns the condition number of $ M$ .
members.detailed: The solution is stored in $ B$ .

PkgSolverInterfaceRef.name: PkgSolverInterfaceRef
PkgSolverInterfaceRef.detailed: Simon Giraudot, Pierre Alliez, Frédéric Cazals, Gaël Guennebaud, Bruno Lévy, Marc Pouget, Laurent Saboret, and Liangliang Nan This package provides concepts and models for solving linear systems with dense or sparse matrices, mixed integer programming problems with or without constraints, and nonlinear programming problems with or without constraints. Introduced in: CGAL 4.8 BibTeX: cgal:eb-solver-26a License: LGPL Classified Reference Pages Concepts DiagonalizeTraits NormalEquationSparseLinearAlgebraTraits_d SparseLinearAlgebraTraits_d SparseLinearAlgebraWithFactorTraits_d SvdTraits MixedIntegerProgramTraits LinearProgramTraits QuadraticProgramTraits Linear Systems CGAL::Eigen_solver_traits CGAL::Eigen_diagonalize_traits CGAL::Eigen_vector CGAL::Eigen_matrix CGAL::Eigen_sparse_matrix CGAL::Eigen_sparse_symmetric_matrix CGAL::Eigen_svd Mixed Integer Programming CGAL::Variable CGAL::Linear_constraint CGAL::Linear_objective CGAL::Mixed_integer_program_traits CGAL::GLPK_mixed_integer_program_traits CGAL::SCIP_mixed_integer_program_traits Nonlinear Programming CGAL::OSQP_quadratic_program_traits
